response.justification: The candidate brings genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests per second in DLang) and hands-on geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS), both directly relevant to routing data pipelines. However, professional C++ experience is limited to a short stint in 2014-2015, there is no Rust, and recent years have been TypeScript/Node product work rather than native pipeline engineering. No direct navigation/routing-tile experience. Worth a conversation if the team can accept a ramp-up on modern C++, but not a clear-cut fit for the primary language requirement.
strengths: Low-latency distributed systems experience (async I/O, caching, fibers, real-time metadata at millions of req/s)
Real geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (2014-2015) and brief; no Rust experience listed
No direct navigation, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles (2020-present) are primarily TypeScript/Node full-stack, away from native systems work
